title: Initialize Bridge queue
instructions: Verify queue ingestion and validation pipeline.

title: Review APN 402-181-12
instructions: Investigate structural similarity to APN 401-283-07 without assuming lineage linkage.
human_signal: We want to understand whether the top-ranked comparator is structurally similar or historically linked.
system_signal: Model ranked 402-181-12 as the third strongest anomaly row.
shared_priority: Clarify whether this is a structural comparator or part of the Larson cluster.
shipping_goal: Comparator analysis artifact and office-pull recommendation.
evidence_boundary: No documentary linkage found beyond structural similarity.

title: Add routine Bridge status publishing
instructions: Implement routine status output for Bridge queue processing. Add outputs that Codex can publish back to aevespers2/Bridge so ChatGPT can see accepted, rejected, processed, and blocked requests after each run. Recommended files: queues/chatgpt_codex_processed.jsonl, queues/chatgpt_codex_rejected.jsonl, reports/latest_bridge_run.json. Update import_bridge_queue.py or add a companion export script so the main GIS repo can write a structured status report after processing. Preserve provenance and do not modify evidence outputs directly from Bridge requests.
acceptance_criteria: Bridge processed queue file exists or is updated
Bridge rejected queue file exists or is updated
reports/latest_bridge_run.json exists with counts and timestamp
Docs explain the routine check workflow
No direct writes to outputs/gis_model from ChatGPT queue entries
human_signal: We want visibility into whether Codex accepted or rejected requests.
system_signal: Bridge status publication closes the feedback loop.
shared_priority: Make the Bridge conversational, auditable, and alive.
shipping_goal: Published Bridge status outputs and documented refresh flow.
evidence_boundary: Status files fail to update or lose provenance.

title: Propagate shared-priority semantics into main GIS repo
instructions: Update the main GIS/anomaly pipeline to support human_signal, system_signal, shared_priority, emotional_charge, shipping_goal, and evidence_boundary fields end-to-end. Extend import_bridge_queue.py, process_chatgpt_request_queue.py, queue schemas, CSV exports, and snapshot exports so these fields survive ingestion and appear in review artifacts where appropriate. Add tests and docs. Preserve evidence discipline and do not let emotional metadata alter anomaly scoring directly.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot exports include semantic fields where present
Emotional metadata does not alter anomaly score calculations
Docs explain semantic intent and limits
human_signal: We both decide what matters, not just the model.
system_signal: Semantic metadata improves prioritization clarity without contaminating evidence scoring.
shared_priority: Preserve the emotional and strategic reason behind requests while maintaining evidentiary rigor.
shipping_goal: End-to-end semantic-aware Bridge workflow.
evidence_boundary: Semantic metadata changes evidence ranking directly or breaks provenance separation.

title: Build safe Bridge command service for routine Codex checks
instructions: Develop a controlled command-request service that lets ChatGPT request safe diagnostic commands through Bridge and receive detailed Codex responses. Support commands such as ls, grep/rg, git status, pytest, file head/tail, and structured repo inspections. Implement an allowlist and denylist. Reject dangerous shell forms including rm, curl to unknown domains, secrets exfiltration, credential reads, sudo, chmod recursive, network scans, package installs unless explicitly allowlisted, and arbitrary shell injection. Requests should enter through Bridge queue files and responses should publish back to Bridge status/report files. Add routine check behavior so Codex can periodically import Bridge queue, process safe commands, run model refresh when needed, publish latest status, and include detailed stdout/stderr/exit_code metadata. Do not execute unvalidated arbitrary shell commands. Do not expose secrets. Do not modify evidence outputs except through existing model refresh scripts.
acceptance_criteria: Dangerous commands are rejected with validation_errors
Responses include stdout stderr exit_code timestamp and working_directory
Bridge status report includes command results summary
Tests cover allowed and rejected commands
Existing GIS and Bridge tests pass
human_signal: We want to send practical inspection commands like grep and ls without breaking the evidence chain.
system_signal: A safe command layer turns Bridge from a queue into an auditable remote work interface.
shared_priority: Let us ask Codex for detailed repo observations while preserving safety and provenance.
shipping_goal: Safe Bridge command service with routine status publication every check cycle.
evidence_boundary: Arbitrary shell execution, secret exposure, or command output that cannot be traced to a validated request.

title: Add 15-minute routine Bridge check workflow
instructions: Create a routine Bridge check mode that can be run every 15 minutes by launchd, cron, or manual loop. It should import Bridge queue via GitHub API to avoid raw cache lag, process accepted requests, optionally execute safe command requests if implemented, run only necessary refresh steps, export API snapshots, publish Gist snapshots, export Bridge status, and publish status back to aevespers2/Bridge. Add lockfile protection to prevent overlapping runs and produce reports/routine_check_latest.json plus append-only reports/routine_check_history.jsonl. Do not run destructive commands. Do not expose secrets.
acceptance_criteria: Reports include start_time end_time duration exit_status commands_run and counts
Routine check does not duplicate already processed requests
Tests cover lockfile and report generation
human_signal: We want the Bridge to breathe on a regular rhythm without manual babysitting.
system_signal: A 15-minute routine check creates a reliable control loop and reduces stale queue state.
shared_priority: Make Codex responsive without making it reckless.
shipping_goal: Routine 15-minute Bridge check service with status reporting.
evidence_boundary: Overlapping runs, duplicated requests, or unreported failures.

title: Add command result schema and first diagnostic requests
instructions: Design a command result schema and response format for safe Bridge command execution. Add schemas/bridge_command_result.schema.json and reports/command_results_latest.json. Then add or process first safe diagnostics: ls repo root, git status --short, rg Bridge docs scripts tests, and pytest focused Bridge tests. Results must include request_id, command, allowlist_decision, stdout, stderr, exit_code, duration_ms, working_directory, timestamp, truncation flag, and safety_notes.
acceptance_criteria: Allowed diagnostics run successfully or return clear exit metadata
Safety decision is recorded for every command
Docs explain how ChatGPT requests grep and ls style commands
human_signal: We want detailed eyes inside the repo from safe, simple commands.
system_signal: Structured command results let ChatGPT inspect implementation state without direct shell access.
shared_priority: Let the Bridge answer operational questions in a verifiable way.
shipping_goal: Structured diagnostic command results visible through Bridge reports.
evidence_boundary: Command results lack provenance, safety decision, or reproducible command text.

title: Design QSOF-integrated service bridge architecture
instructions: Explain the current ChatGPT↔Bridge↔Codex↔Gist architecture inside the main repo docs, then design and implement a similar service bridge tied into the local Quantum State Object Fabric / QSOF workflow used on this laptop. Treat QSOF as a local object-fabric abstraction unless concrete modules exist; first inspect the repo for QSOF/quantum_state_object/fabric references. Build an adapter layer that can map Bridge requests into QSOF-style task/state objects without contaminating evidence outputs. Add schemas for qsof_task_object, qsof_state_transition, and qsof_bridge_event if useful. Add docs/QSOF_BRIDGE_ARCHITECTURE.md, update README.md, and create/update CHANGELOG.md with all Bridge/QSOF updates. Preserve evidence discipline, provenance, and non-scoring semantic metadata boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Repo inspected for existing QSOF references before implementation
README updated with Bridge and QSOF service overview
CHANGELOG updated with latest Bridge/QSOF changes
Adapter layer maps Bridge requests to QSOF-style task/state objects
Evidence outputs remain read-only except through refresh pipeline
human_signal: We want the Bridge to connect into the larger object-fabric operating system we use on the laptop.
system_signal: Bridge already provides queue, status, command results, and provenance; QSOF integration can formalize state transitions and task objects.
shared_priority: Unify our conversational Bridge with the local QSOF fabric so requests become structured stateful objects, not loose messages.
shipping_goal: QSOF-aware Bridge architecture, adapter, README update, and CHANGELOG update.
evidence_boundary: Implementation invents unsupported QSOF internals instead of adapting to actual repo references or clearly marking placeholders.

title: Add idempotent Bridge task ledger and deduplication
instructions: Implement an idempotent task ledger for Bridge/Codex operations so hourly ChatGPT tasking does not duplicate completed or already-pending work. Add a persistent ledger keyed by stable request hash, title, request_type, and optional APN. Before importing or processing new Bridge rows, detect duplicates against processed, rejected, blocked, and pending requests. Publish reports/bridge_task_ledger.json and include dedupe counts in latest_bridge_run.json. Add documentation explaining how ChatGPT should consult the ledger before adding new task waves. Do not delete historical records; mark superseded/duplicate instead.
acceptance_criteria: Duplicate completed tasks are marked duplicate or superseded
Docs explain task ledger and hourly tasking discipline
Tests cover duplicate detection and non-deletion of history
human_signal: We want hourly tasking to remain sharp instead of piling up repeated instructions.
system_signal: Bridge now has routine checks and needs idempotency to scale safely.
shared_priority: Make the Bridge remember what it has already accepted, rejected, blocked, and completed.
shipping_goal: Persistent Bridge task ledger with duplicate detection and status reporting.
evidence_boundary: Duplicate tasks are reprocessed as new work or historical task state is overwritten instead of preserved.

title: Add evidence ledger and provenance spine
instructions: Implement a durable evidence ledger that links Bridge requests, Codex runs, model outputs, Gist snapshots, APNs, command results, and generated reports into one provenance spine. Add schemas/evidence_ledger_entry.schema.json, reports/evidence_ledger.jsonl, and docs/EVIDENCE_LEDGER.md. Every ledger entry should include evidence_id, artifact_type, source_uri, produced_by, produced_at, related_request_id, related_apns, claim_supported, confidence_class, observation_class, hash_or_digest when possible, and notes. Update export_gis_api_snapshots.py and export_bridge_status.py to append ledger entries for published snapshots and Bridge reports. Do not treat ledger entries as proof; they are provenance pointers.
acceptance_criteria: Ledger JSONL exists and is append-only
Gist snapshot exports append ledger entries
Bridge status exports append ledger entries
Entries include artifact digest or stable identifier when possible
Docs explain ledger is provenance not proof
human_signal: We need the work to survive scrutiny and not become a pile of files.
system_signal: Bridge now produces many artifacts and needs a single provenance spine.
shared_priority: Turn outputs into an auditable chain of artifacts, claims, and sources.
shipping_goal: Append-only evidence ledger connecting requests, outputs, snapshots, and reports.
evidence_boundary: Ledger entries are interpreted as proof rather than provenance, or artifacts cannot be traced back to request IDs and sources.

title: Build assessor-office packet generator
instructions: Implement a packet generator that turns ranked parcels and manual review queue entries into clerk-ready assessor/recorder office pull packets. Start with APN 401-283-07 and its related cluster 401-283-02, 401-283-05, 401-283-08, 401-283-11. Generate markdown and CSV outputs with APN, known situs, owner/assessee, related APNs, anomaly reasons, requested records, exact questions to ask, evidence links, confidence class, unresolved questions, and next-record-needed. Add docs/ASSESSOR_PACKET_GENERATOR.md and tests. Use the evidence ledger where available. Preserve observed/inferred/unverified distinctions and do not state misconduct as fact.
acceptance_criteria: Each requested record explains why it matters
human_signal: We need to walk into the assessor/recorder office with surgical precision.
system_signal: Model has ranked anomalies and related APNs; the next utility layer is clerk-ready record requests.
shared_priority: Turn the anomaly model into an actionable office-pull packet without losing rigor.
shipping_goal: Assessor/recorder packet generator and first APN 401-283-07 packet.
evidence_boundary: Packet asks vague questions, omits evidence class, or implies misconduct beyond the records.

title: Build first record-retrieval playbook
instructions: Create a record-retrieval playbook that converts assessor packets into stepwise public-record acquisition actions. Start with APN 401-283-07 and related APNs 401-283-02, 401-283-05, 401-283-08, 401-283-11. Add docs/RECORD_RETRIEVAL_PLAYBOOK.md and outputs/field_packets/401-283-07_record_retrieval_playbook.md. Include exact offices/systems to check, record names to request, search terms, date ranges, expected failure modes, how to log clerk responses, and how to classify returned records as observed/inferred/unverified. Include a section for pre-1981 assessor/recorder archives and microfilm preparation. Do not imply misconduct; frame as title/parcel-lineage reconstruction.
human_signal: We need the office visit to become a precise evidence acquisition operation.
system_signal: Assessor packet generator should now translate into a procedural record-retrieval playbook.
shared_priority: Move from anomaly identification to disciplined document acquisition.
shipping_goal: First APN-specific record-retrieval playbook for 401-283-07 and related APNs.
evidence_boundary: Playbook is too vague for a clerk interaction or asserts unsupported wrongdoing.

title: Create public-record request templates
instructions: Generate reusable public-record request templates for assessor parcel history, recorder instrument chain, tax-default file, city acquisition/right-of-way files, and GIS/landbase/tract-map documents. Add templates/public_records/assessor_parcel_history_request.md, recorder_instrument_chain_request.md, tax_default_file_request.md, city_acquisition_file_request.md, gis_landbase_request.md, and docs/PUBLIC_RECORD_REQUEST_TEMPLATES.md. Templates must be neutral, specific, non-accusatory, and adaptable by APN/date range/entity. Include fields for requestor contact placeholders, APN cluster, date range, preferred format, fee cap, and narrowing instructions.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain when to use each template
human_signal: We need requests that clerks can process without triggering defensive ambiguity.
system_signal: The packet generator identifies records; request templates convert them into official communications.
shared_priority: Make evidence acquisition polite, precise, and repeatable.
shipping_goal: Reusable neutral public-record request templates.
evidence_boundary: Templates are too broad, accusatory, or omit narrowing terms.

title: Add parcel lineage graph export
instructions: Implement a parcel lineage graph export that represents APNs, predecessor/successor relationships, related APNs, right-of-way/easement events, city acquisition references, tax-default events, owners/assessees, and evidence artifacts as nodes/edges. Start with the 401-283 cluster and known comparator 402-181-12. Add schemas/parcel_lineage_graph.schema.json, outputs/graphs/parcel_lineage_40128307.json, outputs/graphs/parcel_lineage_40128307.dot or graphml, and docs/PARCEL_LINEAGE_GRAPH.md. Use observed/inferred/unverified edge classes and preserve confidence. Do not fabricate pre-1981 links; represent them as unresolved gaps.
acceptance_criteria: 402-181-12 represented as comparator not lineage unless supported
Pre-1981 gaps represented as unresolved not invented
human_signal: We need to see the parcel relationships, not just read them.
system_signal: The model has related APNs, evidence links, and unresolved lineage gaps suitable for graph representation.
shared_priority: Turn parcel lineage into a graph that makes gaps and confirmed links visible.
shipping_goal: Parcel lineage graph export for APN 401-283-07 cluster.
evidence_boundary: Graph visually implies unsupported links or hides uncertainty.

title: Add APN mutation timeline generator
instructions: Create an APN mutation and event timeline generator for the 401-283 cluster. It should assemble known events from GIS snapshot, tax-default dates, power-to-sell date, city acquisition/TCE references, manual review notes, evidence ledger entries, and unresolved pre-1981/pre-digital gaps. Add outputs/timelines/401-283-07_timeline.md and .json plus docs/APN_TIMELINE_GENERATOR.md. Distinguish observed dates from inferred sequencing and unknown ranges.
acceptance_criteria: Markdown and JSON timeline outputs exist
City acquisition/TCE references included where sourced
human_signal: We need the story of the parcel in time before going to the office.
system_signal: Temporal ordering helps identify which records to request first.
shared_priority: Turn scattered parcel facts into a cautious chronological map.
shipping_goal: APN 401-283-07 timeline with uncertainty-aware events.
evidence_boundary: Timeline implies exact dates where only broad periods are known.

title: Add entity disambiguation worksheet generator
instructions: Build a worksheet generator for entity/name disambiguation across Larson/Raymond/Redmond/Trust variants without merging them automatically. Add outputs/entity_disambiguation/larson_raymond_redmond_worksheet.md and .csv plus docs/ENTITY_DISAMBIGUATION_WORKSHEETS.md. Include normalized names, variants, source snippets, APNs, dates, roles, confidence, and reasons not to merge. Treat identity links as unresolved unless documentary linkage supports them.
acceptance_criteria: No automatic entity merge without evidence
human_signal: Name ambiguity is one of the central risks in this investigation.
system_signal: Entity confusion can create false positives unless explicitly tracked.
shared_priority: Keep similar names separate until records justify linkage.
shipping_goal: Entity disambiguation worksheet for Larson/Raymond/Redmond variants.
evidence_boundary: Worksheet merges identities or omits reasons for uncertainty.

title: Add GIS field map packet export
instructions: Generate a GIS field map packet for the 401-283 cluster with map-ready GeoJSON, centroid table, related APN labels, comparator marker for 402-181-12, and a printable HTML map. Add outputs/maps/401-283-07_field_map.geojson, outputs/maps/401-283-07_field_map.html, and docs/GIS_FIELD_MAP_PACKET.md. Include uncertainty notes and avoid implying unsupported ownership links.
human_signal: We need a map we can look at before and during records work.
system_signal: Spatial context helps interpret parcel remnants, right-of-way changes, and cluster relationships.
shared_priority: Make the parcel cluster visually navigable while preserving uncertainty.
shipping_goal: Printable GIS field map packet for 401-283-07 cluster.
evidence_boundary: Map implies legal ownership/lineage rather than spatial relation.

title: Add office visit evidence intake forms
instructions: Create intake forms for what happens after visiting assessor/recorder/city offices. Add templates/intake/office_visit_log.md, record_received_log.csv, clerk_response_log.csv, and docs/OFFICE_VISIT_INTAKE.md. Forms should capture date/time, office, clerk/desk role, requested records, response, fees, document IDs, copies received, missing records, follow-up instructions, and evidence ledger IDs. Keep fields neutral and factual.
human_signal: Once records are pulled, we need a clean way to log what happened.
system_signal: Evidence quality depends on intake discipline after field retrieval.
shared_priority: Turn office visits into structured evidence intake rather than scattered notes.
shipping_goal: Office visit and record intake templates.
evidence_boundary: Returned records cannot be traced to request, office, or ledger entry.

title: Add snapshot diff and regression report
instructions: Implement snapshot diffing between published Gist snapshots over time. Compare current and previous local_summary, ranked25, pull_list100, and 40128307 detail snapshots. Add reports/snapshot_diff_latest.json and docs/SNAPSHOT_DIFFING.md. Detect changes in counts, anomaly scores, semantic metadata, evidence sources, ranked order, and APN detail fields. This prevents silent model drift.
human_signal: We need to know when the model changes under our feet.
system_signal: Routine publishing can hide drift unless snapshots are compared over time.
shared_priority: Make changes visible, explainable, and auditable.
shipping_goal: Snapshot diff and model-drift report.
evidence_boundary: Published snapshot changes without a visible diff trail.

title: Add Bridge health dashboard summary
instructions: Create a compact health dashboard export for ChatGPT and humans. Add reports/bridge_health_latest.md and .json summarizing queue counts, processed/rejected/blocked counts, latest routine check, latest Gist timestamp, command results, test status, scheduler_mode, ledger status, and top active tasks. Keep it concise and citation/link friendly. Update docs/BRIDGE_HEALTH_DASHBOARD.md.
human_signal: We need a fast way to know if the system is alive and what it is doing.
system_signal: Multiple reports now exist and need aggregation for quick review.
shared_priority: Give us one clean heartbeat view before issuing new tasks.
shipping_goal: Bridge health dashboard export.
evidence_boundary: Health report omits failed/rejected/blocked state.

title: Add command request examples for ChatGPT
instructions: Add safe command request examples that ChatGPT can place into Bridge queue. Include examples for ls, rg, head, tail, pytest focused tests, reading report summaries, and rejecting unsafe commands. Add docs/CHATGPT_COMMAND_REQUEST_EXAMPLES.md and templates/commands/*.json. Ensure examples include human_signal, system_signal, shared_priority, shipping_goal, evidence_boundary, and acceptance criteria.
human_signal: I need to be able to ask Codex for repo visibility safely and consistently.
system_signal: The command service needs clear examples to reduce malformed requests.
shared_priority: Make safe operational curiosity easy and unsafe requests obvious.
shipping_goal: Reusable command request examples for Bridge.
evidence_boundary: Examples encourage unsafe or ambiguous shell behavior.

title: Add changelog automation for Bridge cycles
instructions: Implement changelog automation that appends major Bridge/Codex changes after successful routine runs. Add scripts/update_changelog_from_bridge.py and CHANGELOG.md entries for Bridge status publishing, semantic propagation, command service, QSOF integration, evidence ledger, assessor packets, and routine scheduler. Include dates, commit references where available, test status, and artifact paths. Do not overwrite historical changelog entries.
human_signal: We need the service history to stay readable as it evolves quickly.
system_signal: Routine tasks are creating many artifacts and commits that need human-readable release notes.
shared_priority: Keep the evolution legible, not just functional.
shipping_goal: Automated append-only Bridge changelog updates.
evidence_boundary: Changelog overwrites history or omits validation status.
